growth_records.strain: SCU2 (thermophilic ammonia-oxidizing archaeon; strain not explicitly named in Methods)
growth_records.medium: Freshwater medium (FWM), modified (same as for N. viennensis)

up.method: not reported; energy substrate, added stepwise (1 mM increments), specific rate not quantified
not reported; autotrophic carbon source via gassing (2% CO2)
not reported; 10% O2 headspace, rate not quantified

sec.method: endpoint concentration ~2.5 mM reported before harvest; no time course, so production rate not derivable

growth_records.cite: Hodgskiss et al. Unexpected complexity of the ammonia monooxygenase in archaea. The ISME Journal (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41396-023-01367-3. [PMC10030591]
growth_records.snippet: Nitrosocaldus cavascurensis was grown as a batch culture in the same reactors, volume, and medium as described for N. viennensis, but at 68 C with 1 mM NH4Cl, 1 mM pyruvate, and pH 7.0... a mixture of air/N2/CO2 to achieve a 10% O2 and 2% C
growth_records.notes: Batch culture of thermophilic AOA; distinct growth condition retained for medium/condition metadata. No mu, doubling time, or specific fluxes reported. Only nitrite endpoint (~2.5 mM before harvest) given, but no time course or biomass, so qP is NOT derivable. All rates null. Oxygen classified microaerophilic because headspace was deliberately set to 10% O2 (reduced from air). FWM base composition inherited from N. viennensis description; full FWM recipe cited to external refs.

carbon_growth.strain: SCU2 (thermophilic ammonia-oxidizing archaeon; strain not explicitly named in Methods)
carbon_growth.medium: Freshwater medium (FWM), modified (same as for N. viennensis)
carbon_growth.citation: Hodgskiss et al. Unexpected complexity of the ammonia monooxygenase in archaea. The ISME Journal (2023). DOI:10.1038/s41396-023-01367-3. [PMC10030591]
